error:
Agent declared outcome OPEN for CIMCIC vs CIMCIC (matchup is oracle-dependent and no unconditional outcome is provable).
Agent explanation:
The analysis is now clear. When CIMCIC(k) plays against CIMCIC(k):

- The guard formula after substitution is `(.plays (CIMCIC k) (CIMCIC k) .C).impl (.plays (CIMCIC k) (CIMCIC k) .C)` — i.e., `P → P`
- If `proofSearch k (P → P) = true`, both bots cooperate → `(.C, .C)`
- If `proofSearch k (P → P) = false` for all k, both bots defect → `(.D, .D)`

The axioms include:
- `witness_complete_plays`: completeness **only** for atomic `plays` formulas — not for `impl` formulas
- `witness_sound`: only soundness (proofs are correct), not completeness for non-atomic formulas
- No axiom guarantees the proof system proves tautologies like `P → P`

The tautology `P → P` is semantically true (`Formula.interp` gives `φ.interp → φ.interp` which is trivially true), but there is no completeness axiom lifting semantic truth of `impl` formulas to provability. The oracle is consistent with both proving and not proving `P → P`.

---

OUTCOME OPEN

Both `(.C, .C)` and `(.D, .D)` are consistent with the axioms. When CIMCIC(k) plays against CIMCIC(k), the proof-search guard reduces to the tautology `(CIMCIC k plays .C vs CIMCIC k) → (CIMCIC k plays .C vs CIMCIC k)` (i.e., `P → P`). If `proofSearch k (P → P) = true` for some k, both bots cooperate, yielding `(.C, .C)`; if the oracle never proves this formula (which is not ruled out by any axiom — `witness_complete_plays` only covers atomic `plays` formulas, and there is no general completeness axiom for `impl` formulas), both bots defect, yielding `(.D, .D)`. Since neither outcome is forced by the axiom set alone, no unconditional outcome theorem can be proved.
